subject: A red war banner mounted on a long spear-pole, planted upright on a battlefield ridge with smoke and rubble behind it
description: A tall red banner streams from a spear-tipped pole driven into the rocky ground of a battlefield. The flag is a deep crimson with a darker emblem or stain across its surface, and it whips to the left as if caught in hot wind. Behind the banner, smoke billows from the wreckage of a recent battle — broken weapons, shattered shields, and dust catch the warm orange glow of fires offscreen. The horizon is hazy with ash, the palette dominated by reds, blacks, and smoldering oranges. No figures are present; the banner stands as the sole heraldic subject, claiming the ground.
foreground: red war banner on a spear-pole planted in rocky earth, surrounded by battlefield debris
background: smoke-choked battlefield ridge with broken weapons and ash-hazed sky lit by distant fires
emotion: defiant, claimed-ground stillness; the banner reads as a victorious or defiant marker after slaughter
